frame_analyses.description: Title card: 'DUNDEE KNOT' by DR RK MISHRA, World Laparoscopy Hospital
Two hands in orange gloves holding purple and tan suture material in extended position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates basic suture handling setup; hands relaxed, suture straight

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating purple suture loop and tan suture into wrapping configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear demonstration of initial loop formation; left hand (viewer's perspective) manipulating loop structure

frame_analyses.description: Both hands engaged; purple suture wraps around tan suture axis; hands positioned for tension application
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand (surgeon's view) leading the wrap in a methodical, slow manner to demonstrate motion

frame_analyses.description: Hands repositioning; tan suture being drawn through purple loop; smooth controlled motion
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper suture draw-through angle and tension; clean wrap execution

frame_analyses.description: Completed first throw visible; purple knot segment cinched around tan suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw (surgeon's knot) configuration evident; right hand led the first wrap

frame_analyses.description: Hands repositioning for second throw; left hand (surgeon's view) now leading; purple loop reengaging with tan suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand switch demonstration: right hand led throw 1, now left hand leads throw 2

frame_analyses.description: Left hand wrapping purple suture around tan suture axis; single wrap motion
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slow, deliberate single throw; demonstrates proper wrapping technique without acceleration

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completion; tan suture pulled through purple loop; second knot segment visible below first
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw; appropriate tension; knot remains secure

frame_analyses.description: Hand repositioning; right hand (surgeon's view) now leading again; purple loop reengages
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch from throw 2 (left led) to throw 3 (right leads); demonstrates alternating pattern

frame_analyses.description: Right hand wrapping third knot; single wrap execution; smooth and controlled
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw of sequence; maintains tension control; knot integrity preserved

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot with three throws visible; both suture ends (purple and tan) extending from cinched knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All three throws complete and secure; ready for suture cutting phase

penalties.description: No suture deviation, gap, knot failure, drain avulsion, or hand-switch violations observed in demonstration

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with proper wrap and draw-through; tension appropriate; no slippage
Proper hand switch from throw 1 (right) to throw 2 (left); single wrap clean and secure
Proper hand switch from throw 2 (left) to throw 3 (right); alternating pattern maintained throughout sequence

suture_placement.notes: Expert demonstration does not show suture passing through actual marked points on trainer; inferred as perfect placement for instructional clarity

drain_assessment.notes: Demonstration uses loose suture material without actual Penrose drain; not applicable to score but technique is shown

score_components.note: Score computed for reference; this is an expert demonstration, not a trainee performance under standard FLS conditions

confidence_rationale: Expert demonstration video with clear, well-lit frames showing each phase transition. Knot technique, hand switching, and suture handling are explicitly visible. Completion time is approximate as this is instructional content without formal task timing. All technical elements (three throws, proper hand switching, knot security) are demonstrated correctly and observable with high confidence.
cannot_determine: Exact suture deviation in mm — demonstration does not show actual FLS trainer with marked entry/exit points
Drain slit closure quality — no actual Penrose drain present in demonstration
Actual task completion time under FLS proficiency testing — this is slowed instructional presentation
video_classification_rationale: Classified as 'expert_demo' because: (1) titled 'DUNDEE KNOT' by Dr. RK Mishra, World Laparoscopy Hospital; (2) slow, deliberate technique demonstration; (3) frames focus on hand position and knot formation; (4) no actual FLS trainer box or marked drain visible; (5) clearly intended to teach technique rather than assess trainee performance.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper suture handling grip and control
Surgeon's knot formation (double throw) with leading hand (right) first
Single throw second throw with left hand (hand switch)
Single throw third throw with right hand (hand switch back)
Alternating hand pattern for all three throws
Tension control to prevent knot slippage
Progressive knot building with visible segments after each throw
verbal_cues: Creator attribution: DR RK MISHRA, World Laparoscopy Hospital (credibility cue)
Frame overlays: 'Dundee Jamming knot' (teaching label on multiple frames)
common_errors_addressed: Maintains consistent hand switching pattern — avoids the error of leading every throw with same hand
Demonstrates controlled tension — avoids loose or slack knot (knot slippage error)
Shows proper wrap configuration — avoids improper loop formation that could lead to knot failure
Maintains knot security throughout sequence — avoids the error of knot unraveling between throws
teaching_content.teaching_quality: High-quality instructional video with slow, deliberate motions allowing frame-by-frame analysis of technique. Ideal for teaching proper knot-tying sequence and hand alternation pattern.

technique_summary: Expert-level demonstration of intracorporeal suture knot tying using the 'Dundee Knot' (also called 'Dundee Jamming Knot') technique by Dr. R.K. Mishra. The video showcases flawless execution: surgeon's knot (double throw) led by right hand, followed by proper hand switches to left hand for throw 2 and back to right for throw 3. All throws are clean, tension is controlled, and knot security is maintained throughout. No technical errors are demonstrated.
strengths: Perfect hand alternation across all three throws
Smooth, controlled wrapping motion without jerking or hesitation
Clear, well-lit presentation allowing detailed observation of knot formation
Appropriate tension management preventing knot slippage
Demonstrable progression of knot building with visible segments after each throw
Expert-level speed and efficiency in knot formation
No wasted motion or repositioning between throws
improvement_suggestions: This is an expert demonstration — no improvement suggestions applicable
